name: Chorizo & Mozzarella Arancini
subtitle: Lemon & Thyme Aioli · Romesco
label: Arancini - Large
method_steps: Sauté finely diced brown onion over medium heat until golden. Add crushed garlic and chorizo, cook 1–2 minutes until chorizo browns. Deglaze with white wine.
Add rice, spicy capsicum dip, onion/garlic/chorizo mix to a deep bain. Cover with water and chicken stock concentrate, salt and pepper. Stir to combine.
Cover bain with lid. Bake at 160°C for 20 minutes, stir, then bake another 20 minutes until water is fully absorbed.
Transfer rice to a bain-marie and refrigerate (or freeze) until fully cooled.
Grate all three cheeses. Stir into cooled rice along with diced roasted capsicum until fully incorporated.
Weigh rice into 50g portions and roll into balls.
Set up coating station: beaten eggs, GF flour, GF breadcrumbs. Roll balls in flour, dip in egg, coat thoroughly in breadcrumbs.
Place on parchment-lined tray and freeze until solid. Vacuum seal (SEAL ONLY — do not vacuum to avoid squashing). Defrost in fridge 24 hours before service.
service: Deep fry arancini until heated through — check with skewer. Season immediately with salt flakes.
Warm Romesco on stove or in microwave.
Place ½ cup warm Romesco as base on each plate. Arrange 3 arancini on top.
Add a dot of Lemon Thyme Aioli on each arancini. Garnish with freshly cut microherbs.
note: DO NOT vacuum seal — seal only. Defrost minimum 24 hours in fridge before frying.

name: Calamari Fritti
subtitle: Lemon Dill Aioli · Crispy Capers
label: Calamari
method_steps: STORAGE: When calamari and octopus arrive, portion immediately — 15 pieces calamari + 3 baby octopus per 500ml PC. Label, store in freezer crate labelled Calamari.
FLOUR MIX: Combine rice flour, GF corn flour, salt, pepper and lemon pepper seasoning. Mix thoroughly, transfer to labelled PCs, store in fridge.
CAPERS: Drain thoroughly and pat dry with chux. Store in PC lined with chux to absorb excess moisture. Refrigerate.
service: Heat oil in fryers. Fry capers 30 seconds – 1 minute until crispy. Drain on chux-lined bowl.
Coat calamari strips and baby octopus evenly in flour mix.
Fry calamari immediately for ~1 minute until crispy. Do not overcrowd basket.
Season with pinch of salt while hot.
TAPAS: Base of 1–2 tbsp lemon dill aioli. Arrange calamari and baby octopus on top. Sprinkle crispy capers, garnish with microgreens.
CANAPE: Small dot of aioli in bamboo boat. Calamari on top, finish with crispy capers.

name: Kilpatrick Oysters
subtitle: Crispy Speck · Lea & Perrins · Rock Salt
label: Oysters Kilpatrick
method_steps: OYSTERS: Remove from freezer 24 hours before event and defrost in fridge. Finely dice speck into PC, refrigerate.
KILPATRICK SAUCE: Mix GF Worcestershire, BBQ sauce, tabasco and dried parsley. Decant into squeezy bottle and label.
GARNISH: Slice lemons, store in labelled PC in fridge.
service: Bunch aluminium foil into a bain tray so oysters can be pressed in and held stable.
Arrange oysters on foil-lined tray. Add 1–2 tsp Kilpatrick sauce onto each oyster. Add 1 tsp diced speck per oyster.
Bake at 180°C for 10 minutes or until golden.
Spread rock salt ~1 inch thick on serving tray. Place baked oysters on salt. Garnish with rocket/microgreens and lemon slices.

name: Slow Cooked Veal Meatballs
subtitle: Romesco Sugo · Toasted Focaccia · Gremolata
label: Meatballs
method_steps: Blitz onion and garlic in food processor. Mix into breadcrumbs — squeeze so onion juices wet the crumbs.
Combine breadcrumb mix with veal mince, parmesan, cream, salt, pepper and herbs. Mix thoroughly.
Wet hands to prevent sticking. Roll mixture into 80g balls.
Place meatballs in deep bain, cover generously with romesco sauce.
Bake in preheated oven at 150°C for 1 hour. Allow to cool in sauce before portioning.
Store in round or rectangle PCs in serves of 3. Distribute romesco across all serves. Label and freeze in crate labelled Meatballs.
service: Reheat in microwave. Check internal temperature and continue heating if needed.
Place reheated meatballs and sauce in medium serving bowl.
Toast 3× 2cm thick focaccia slices until golden on both sides.
Top with thinly grated Manchego and gremolata.

name: Lemon Pepper Chicken Skewer
subtitle: Herbed Labneh · Crumbled Feta · Microherbs
label: Chicken Skewers
method_steps: Combine oil, lemon pepper, limoncello, lemon juice/zest, honey and thyme. Dice chicken into 1-inch pieces and coat in marinade.
Thread 3 pieces per 12cm wooden skewer. Refrigerate overnight in bain-marie if possible. Store in 500ml PCs (1 serve = 3 skewers), freeze.
LABNEH: Line holy bain with 3× chux. Add yogurt on top, cover with more chux. Hang overnight in fridge.
Once hung, mix labneh with finely chopped parsley, dill, mint, lemon zest and finely diced cucumber (no seeds). Season with lemon juice, salt and pepper. Refrigerate.
service: Preheat oven to 180°C.
Lay skewers flat on tray (not overlapping). Roast 10 minutes.
Place a heaped tablespoon of herbed labneh in centre of plate. Arrange 3 skewers. Add crumbled feta and green microherb garnish.

name: Crispy Fried Chorizo Potatoes
subtitle: Lemon Thyme Aioli · Shallots
label: Chorizo Potatoes
method_steps: Bake potatoes at 180°C for 35–40 minutes until soft. Cool.
Smash cooled potatoes into uneven pieces — aim for 6–8 pieces per large potato.
Store potato pieces in labelled PCs, refrigerate.
Slice chorizo lengthwise then cut 5mm wide at 45° angle. Slice shallots thinly at 45°. Store separately in labelled PCs.
service: Deep fry cooled potato pieces until crispy.
Add chorizo slices to basket and cook 20–30 seconds.
Transfer to large metal bowl. Add mayo and sliced shallots, season to taste, toss to coat.
Serve 4–5 pieces in bamboo boats with bamboo fork.

name: Chargrilled Lamb Cutlet
subtitle: Riviera House Emulsion · Rocket
label: Lamb Cutlets
method_steps: French the cutlets. Season generously with salt and pepper.
Two days before event: place in bain lined with clean chux, cover, label, refrigerate.
service: Heat large frying pan. Sear each side of cutlets until nicely coloured.
Transfer to bain, bake in oven 10 minutes at 180°C.
Lay bed of rocket on small plate. Arrange 3 cutlets neatly on top.
Generously drizzle Riviera House Emulsion over cutlets, ensuring each is well-coated.
note: Season cutlets two days before event for best flavour development.

name: Crispy Reef Fish Slider
subtitle: Lemon Caper Aioli · Rocket · Brioche
label: Fish Slider
method_steps: Defrost fish in fridge 2 days before event. Cut into ~70g pieces the day before.
BATTER: Mix flour, onion powder, garlic powder, salt, pepper. Whisk in cold sparkling water until pancake-batter consistency. Add vodka and mix to combine.
PART COOK: Preheat deep fryer. Dip fish in batter, shake off excess, fry in batches of 5–6 for ~1 minute to seal batter only. Drain on wire rack.
Store part-cooked fish in bain-marie lined with baking paper, single layer. Wrap, label, refrigerate.
LEMON CAPER AIOLI: Strain and dry capers, zest lemons. Combine capers, lemon zest, mayo, salt and pepper. Adjust seasoning, refrigerate in PC.
BRIOCHE: Remove from freezer, separate tops/bottoms, store in fridge wrapped in glad wrap.
service: Warm brioche at 170°C for 10 minutes.
Finish frying part-cooked fish until golden and crispy.
Place brioche base on platter, add small handful of rocket. Place fish on rocket. Add generous amount of lemon caper aioli.
Top with brioche lid, secure with skewer.

name: Beef Kofta
subtitle: Spicy Capsicum Sauce · Pita · Rocket
label: Beef Kofta
method_steps: Sauté finely diced onion and garlic until translucent.
Combine beef mince, browned onion/garlic, finely chopped mint, cumin, coriander, cinnamon, pepper, paprika and salt. Mix thoroughly.
Fry 1 tbsp of mix to taste. Adjust seasoning.
Roll mince into 50g balls, shape into football shape.
Lay in bain-marie lined with baking paper. Cover and freeze.
Vacuum seal 40 koftas per bag. Label and return to freezer. Defrost 24 hours before use.
PITA: Defrost 24 hours ahead. Cut in half, line in bain-marie, cover and refrigerate.
Finely chop parsley and mint, store in labelled PC.
service: Bake koftas in bain at 180°C for 20 minutes.
Warm pita at 170°C for 10 minutes.
Open pita, place in large bamboo boat. Add small amount of rocket, then a kofta.
Top with dollop of spicy capsicum sauce and chopped herbs. Arrange neatly on platter.

name: Camembert, Pecan & Cranberry Cigars
subtitle: Thyme Infused Honey · Microherbs
label: Camembert Cigars
method_steps: Melt butter. Cut camembert into 1cm thick × 10cm long sticks. Finely chop pecans and cranberries.
Lay 2 sheets filo on clean surface, brush top with melted butter. Cut sheets in half lengthwise, then each half into 3 long strips.
Place cheese on narrow end of each strip, add 1 tsp pecan/cranberry mix. Fold sides in and roll tightly like a spring roll.
Freeze on parchment-lined tray until solid. Vacuum seal for storage. Defrost in fridge 24 hours before use.
HONEY: Combine honey, thyme, salt and cinnamon stick in pot. Warm over low heat, simmer 10 minutes. Transfer to PC and allow to continue infusing.
service: Deep fry cigars ~2 minutes until golden brown.
Arrange on platter. Drizzle with thyme-infused honey.
Garnish with chopped pecans, cranberries, microgreens and edible flowers.

name: Romesco
subtitle: House Sauce
label: Romesco
method_steps: Heat olive oil in pan. Sauté onions and garlic until translucent.
Blend sautéed onions/garlic with capsicum, passata, basil, salt, pepper and a pinch of sugar until smooth.
Pour into large pot and simmer 1.5–2 hours until flavours develop. Taste and adjust.
Portion into PCs, label and freeze. Remove from freezer as needed.
service: Heat on stovetop or microwave until hot. Use as base or accompany as needed.

name: Lemon Dill Aioli
subtitle: House component
label: Lemon Dill Aioli
method_steps: Finely chop dill and place in a bowl.
Remove lemon rind with microplane, juice lemons.
Combine dill, lemon zest, mayonnaise, salt and pepper in a bowl. Mix well.
Taste and adjust seasoning. Transfer to a PC, label and refrigerate.
service: Serve with Calamari Fritti (tapas or canapé) or as needed.

name: Lemon Thyme Aioli
subtitle: House component
label: Lemon Thyme Aioli
method_steps: Pick thyme leaves into a bowl.
Remove lemon rind with microplane, juice lemons.
Combine thyme leaves, lemon zest, mayonnaise, salt and pepper in a bowl. Mix well.
Taste and adjust seasoning. Transfer to a PC, label and refrigerate.
service: Serve with arancini, chorizo potatoes, or as needed.

name: Vodka Sauce
subtitle: Uses Romesco
label: Vodka Sauce
method_steps: Add all ingredients to a pot. Simmer on the stove until thick and rich.
Label and store as needed.
service: Heat and use as a sauce base or accompaniment.

name: Whipped Butter
subtitle: Bread service
label: Whipped Butter
method_steps: Whip butter until light, white and fluffy.
Place in a piping bag with large star nozzle. Pipe onto small plates and refrigerate.
service: Serve with breads.

name: Riviera House Emulsion
subtitle: House Sauce
label: Emulsion
method_steps: Peel lemon rind with potato peeler, juice lemons. Trim coriander roots, roughly chop all herbs.
In food processor, combine apricots, dill, coriander, parsley, lemon juice/zest, red wine vinegar, olive oil, jalapeños, garlic, castor sugar, salt and pepper.
Blend until smooth. Taste and adjust salt/sugar to balance.
Transfer to PC, label and refrigerate. For bulk: add olive oil over top before freezing to prevent freezer burn.
service: Serve cold or room temperature. Drizzle generously over protein.
note: Add extra olive oil on top before freezing to prevent freezer burn.
